title: Finding the Subarray with Minimum Sum of Size K

content.problemStatement:
You are given an array of integers and you need to find a subarray of size KKK that has the minimum sum in all the subarray of size KKK.


Input Format

The first line contains two integers NNN and KKK, representing the size of the array and the size of the subarray, respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers representing the elements of the array.


Output Format
Output a single integer representing the minimum sum of a subarray of size KKK.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers N and K (1 ≤ K ≤ N). The second line contains N space‑separated integers A[i] (|A[i]| ≤ 10⁹).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer – the minimum possible sum of any contiguous subarray of size K.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first three elements (1,2,3) give the smallest sum 6.
Subarray [1,2] (positions 4 and 5) has sum 3, the minimum.
The first four elements (1,2,3,4) sum to 10, the smallest possible.

solution.approach: The sum of a subarray of length K can be updated in O(1) when the window slides by one position: subtract the element leaving the window and add the new element entering it. Initialise the sum of the first K elements, store it as the current best, then slide the window from left to right updating the sum and the best whenever a smaller sum is found. This linear scan processes each element exactly once, giving O(N) time per test case and O(1) extra space besides the input array.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing the array (can be O(1) if streamed) – only a few 64‑bit variables besides the input.

code.Python3:
import sys
def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it)); K = int(next(it))
        arr = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        cur = sum(arr[:K])
        best = cur
        for i in range(K, N):
            cur += arr[i] - arr[i-K]
            if cur < best:
                best = cur
        out.append(str(best))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))
if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
